fsm_model.description: Password-authenticated key exchange protocol for establishing first secure session between Commissioner (Initiator) and Commissionee (Responder) using out-of-band passcode. This comprehensive FSM includes all timeout handling, message deduplication, transient states, enhanced failure modes, message validation, and security properties.

roles.description: Commissioner that initiates PASE session using known passcode (derived w0, w1 values)
Commissionee that responds with PAKE verifier (w0, L) and establishes session

states.description: No active PASE session establishment
invariants: Initiator has passcode (or will derive w0, w1)
Responder has PAKE verifier (w0, L) for passcode

states.description: Initiator has sent PBKDF parameter request and awaits response
invariants: HasPBKDFParameters flag set (True if salt/iterations known)
Message sent via unsecured session (Session Type = 0, Session ID = 0)
S Flag = 1, DSIZ = 0 in message header

states.description: Initiator received PBKDF parameters and is ready to compute w0/w1
invariants: ResponderSessionId received and stored as Peer Session Identifier
PBKDF parameters received (salt, iterations) if HasPBKDFParameters was False
Crypto_PAKEValues_Initiator ready to be computed: (w0, w1) = PBKDF(passcode, salt, iterations)
Transition to CryptoPBKDFComputing for actual computation

states.description: Initiator computing w0/w1 from passcode via PBKDF (may take 100ms-10s)
invariants: UI may show progress indicator for long computations
states.computation_note: Duration depends on iteration count: 1000 iterations ~50ms, 10000 ~500ms, 100000 ~5-10s

states.description: Initiator sent pA and awaits Pake2 with pB and cB
invariants: pA computed and sent (CRYPTO_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE_BYTES, uncompressed format)
Awaiting Pake2 with pB and cB

states.description: Responder validated PBKDFParamRequest and sent response
invariants: ResponderSessionId stored as Local Session Identifier
InitiatorSessionId stored as Peer Session Identifier
PBKDF parameters sent if HasPBKDFParameters == False
S Flag = 0, DSIZ = 1 in response message

states.description: Responder received pA and is computing pB, transcript TT, and cA/cB/Ke
invariants: Crypto_PAKEValues_Responder available: (w0, L) where L = w1 * P
Ready to compute pB = Crypto_pB(w0, L)
Ready to compute transcript TT = Crypto_Transcript(PBKDFParamRequest, PBKDFParamResponse, pA, pB)
Ready to compute (cA, cB, Ke) = Crypto_P2(TT, pA, pB)
Transition to CryptoPAKEComputing for actual computation

states.description: Responder computing pB, TT, cA, cB, Ke via SPAKE2+ (10-50ms)
invariants: w0, L available from PAKE verifier
Computing: Z = (pA - w0*M) * y where pB = y*P + N
states.computation_note: ECC operations typically 10-50ms depending on hardware

states.description: Responder sent pB and cB, has derived Ke, awaiting Pake3 with cA
invariants: cA computed but not yet verified (awaiting from initiator)
Session keys NOT yet activated (waiting for Pake3 verification)
Pake2 message cached for duplicate handling

states.description: Initiator received pB and cB, computing Ke, verifying cB
invariants: TT computed: Crypto_Transcript(PBKDFParamRequest, PBKDFParamResponse, pA, pB)
(cA, cB_computed, Ke) computed via Crypto_P2(TT, pA, pB)
If verification fails: send FAILURE StatusReport and abort
If verification succeeds: send Pake3 with cA

states.description: Initiator sent cA after successful cB verification, has Ke, awaiting PakeFinished
invariants: Session keys derivable: I2RKey || R2IKey || AttestationChallenge = KDF(Ke, [], 'SessionKeys', 3*CRYPTO_SYMMETRIC_KEY_LENGTH_BITS)
MUST NOT send encrypted data until PakeFinished received
Pake3 message cached for duplicate handling

states.description: Responder received cA and is verifying it
invariants: Must verify: cA_received == cA_computed (CONSTANT-TIME!) (from Pake2_Sent state)
If verification fails: send FAILURE StatusReport and abort
If verification succeeds: set SessionTimestamp and send PakeFinished

states.description: Both parties deriving I2RKey, R2IKey, AttestationChallenge from Ke (1-5ms)
PASE session successfully established with encryption keys derived
invariants: Local Message Counter initialized per Section 4.6.1.1
Message Reception State initialized with peer max_message_counter = 0
Initiator encrypts with I2RKey, decrypts with R2IKey
Responder encrypts with R2IKey, decrypts with I2RKey

states.description: PASE failed due to invalid PasscodeId
PASE failed because initiator could not verify cB from Pake2
invariants: All cryptographic state cleared (w0, w1, pA, pB, Ke)

states.description: PASE failed because responder could not verify cA from Pake3
invariants: All cryptographic state cleared (w0, L, pB, Ke, cA, cB)

states.description: PASE failed due to cryptographic computation error
invariants: Computation failure occurred (ECC point validation, iteration overflow, etc.)
error_types: pA equals point M or N (attack detection)
pB equals point M or N (attack detection)

states.description: PASE failed due to message format error
invariants: TLV parsing error or missing required fields
error_types: Invalid field size (pA not 65 bytes, random not 32 bytes)

states.description: PASE failed due to session ID already in use
invariants: Proposed Session ID conflicts with existing PASE or CASE session

states.description: PASE failed due to PBKDF parameter validation failure
invariants: PBKDF parameters invalid or out of bounds
error_types: Iteration count mismatch with QR code

states.description: PASE failed due to message timeout
invariants: Expected message not received within timeout period

guard: No existing PASE session for this Commissionee
Rate limit not exceeded (max 3 failures per minute)
actions: InitiatorSessionId := Choose unique session ID (16-bit, non-overlapping)
Verify InitiatorSessionId not in use (else choose different ID)
HasPBKDFParameters := True if (salt, iterations) known from QR code, else False
Construct PBKDFParamRequest TLV: {initiatorRandom, initiatorSessionId, passcodeId, hasPBKDFParameters, [initiatorSessionParams]}
Set Message Header: Session Type := 0 (unsecured), Session ID := 0, S Flag := 1, DSIZ := 0
Set Protocol Header: I Flag := 1, PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL, Opcode := PBKDFParamRequest
transitions.security_notes: Unsecured message (no encryption yet), relies on passcode secrecy for PAKE security

guard: PAKE verifier (w0, L) available for commissioning window passcode
No existing PASE session with this initiator
InitiatorSessionId not already in use locally
actions: Validate message header (Session Type = 0, Session ID = 0, S Flag = 1)
Verify InitiatorSessionId not colliding with existing sessions
ResponderSessionId := Choose unique session ID (16-bit, non-overlapping)
If PBKDFParamRequest.hasPBKDFParameters == False: Include PBKDF params (salt, iterations) in response
Construct PBKDFParamResponse TLV: {initiatorRandom_echo, responderRandom, responderSessionId, pbkdf_parameters, [responderSessionParams]}
Set Message Header: Session Type := 0 (unsecured), Session ID := 0, S Flag := 0, DSIZ := 1
Set Protocol Header: I Flag := 0, PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL, Opcode := PBKDFParamResponse
transitions.security_notes: Echo InitiatorRandom for transcript integrity; PBKDF params sent in clear but not security-sensitive

actions: Send StatusReport: GeneralCode := FAILURE, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := INVALID_PARAMETER
transitions.security_notes: Prevents PASE with unsupported passcode identifiers

guard: Message header valid (Session Type = 0, S Flag = 0, DSIZ = 1)
initiatorRandom echo matches sent value (CONSTANT-TIME comparison)
PBKDF parameters received and valid (if HasPBKDFParameters was False)
actions: Validate message format and TLV structure
Verify ResponderSessionId not already in use locally
Extract PBKDF parameters: (salt, iterations) from response
Transition to CryptoPBKDFComputing to compute w0, w1
transitions.security_notes: Echo validation prevents reflection attacks

actions: w0 := w0s mod p (elliptic curve order)
Compute pA := Crypto_pA(w0, w1) [SPAKE2+ point computation: pA = w0*P + M]
Verify pA is valid EC point (on curve, not at infinity)
Verify pA != M, pA != N, pA != P (attack prevention)
Set Protocol Header: I Flag := 1, PROTOCOL_ID_SECURE_CHANNEL, Opcode := Pake1
transitions.timing_requirements: After PBKDF computation (100ms-10s depending on iterations)
transitions.security_notes: pA leaks no info about passcode due to SPAKE2+ blinding with point M

guard: PBKDF computation failed (timeout, overflow, invalid parameters)
actions: Send StatusReport: GeneralCode := FAILURE, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := INVALID_PARAMETER
transitions.security_notes: Must clear all cryptographic state to prevent leakage

guard: pA is valid EC point (on curve, not at infinity)
pA != 0, pA != P, pA != M, pA != N
Crypto_PAKEValues_Responder available: (w0, L) where L = w1 * P
actions: Extract pA from Pake1 (65 bytes, uncompressed)
Verify pA is valid point on P-256 curve
Verify pA != M, pA != N (SPAKE2+ blinding points)
Transition to CryptoPAKEComputing for SPAKE2+ operations
transitions.security_notes: pA validation prevents small-subgroup attacks

guard: pB, cA, cB, Ke values derived
actions: Compute pB := Crypto_pB(w0, L) [SPAKE2+ point computation: pB = w0*P + N]
Verify pB is valid EC point
Verify pB != M, pB != N, pB != P (attack prevention)
Compute Context := Crypto_Hash('CHIP PAKE V1 Commissioning' || PBKDFParamRequest || PBKDFParamResponse)
Compute Z, V from pA, pB per SPAKE2+ (shared secrets from elliptic curve DH)
Compute TT := Crypto_Transcript(PBKDFParamRequest, PBKDFParamResponse, pA, pB)
TT includes: Context, M, N, pA, pB, Z, V, w0 (see Section 3.10.3)
Compute (cA, cB, Ke) := Crypto_P2(TT, pA, pB) using SPAKE2+ confirmation computation
cA := HMAC(KcA, pB), cB := HMAC(KcB, pA)
Ke derived from shared secret (CRYPTO_HASH_LEN_BYTES/2 = 16 bytes)
Set Protocol Header: I Flag := 0, Opcode := Pake2
transitions.security_notes: cB proves responder knows passcode; Ke is shared key basis

guard: SPAKE2+ computation failed (invalid EC point, etc.)
actions: Clear all cryptographic state (w0, L, pA, partial results)
Send StatusReport: GeneralCode := FAILURE, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := INVALID_PARAMETER
transitions.security_notes: Must clear all cryptographic state to prevent leakage

guard: pB and cB extracted and valid
pB is valid EC point (on curve, not at infinity)
pB != 0, pB != P, pB != M, pB != N
actions: Validate pB format (65 bytes, uncompressed, 0x04 prefix)
Validate pB is valid EC point on P-256
Validate pB != M, pB != N (attack prevention)
Compute Context := Crypto_Hash('CHIP PAKE V1 Commissioning' || PBKDFParamRequest || PBKDFParamResponse)
Compute Z, V from pA, pB per SPAKE2+ (shared secrets)
Compute TT := Crypto_Transcript(PBKDFParamRequest, PBKDFParamResponse, pA, pB)
Compute (cA, cB_computed, Ke) := Crypto_P2(TT, pA, pB)
If verification fails: transition to Failed_cB_Verification
Set Protocol Header: I Flag := 1, Opcode := Pake3
DO NOT send encrypted data yet (must wait for PakeFinished)
transitions.security_notes: cB verification proves responder has correct passcode; cA proves initiator has it. MUST use constant-time comparison!

guard: cB verification failed: Pake2.cB != computed cB
actions: Send StatusReport: GeneralCode := FAILURE, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := INVALID_PARAMETER
Clear all cryptographic state (w0, w1, pA, pB, Ke candidates)
Securely zeroize memory containing passcode-derived values
transitions.security_notes: Prevents session establishment with wrong passcode; MUST NOT leak timing information about which byte mismatched

guard: Previously computed cA available from Pake2_Sent state
actions: If verification fails: transition to Failed_cA_Verification
If verification succeeds: transition to T7_EstablishSession
transitions.security_notes: cA verification proves initiator has correct passcode before session activation. MUST use constant-time comparison!

guard: cA verification failed: Pake3.cA != computed cA
actions: Send StatusReport: GeneralCode := FAILURE, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := INVALID_PARAMETER
Clear all cryptographic state (w0, L, pB, Ke, cA, cB)
Securely zeroize memory containing verifier-derived values
transitions.security_notes: Prevents rogue initiator from establishing session with wrong passcode. MUST NOT leak timing information!

actions: Set SessionTimestamp := current time (for session aging)
Transition to KeyDerivationComputing (or inline if fast):
Derive session keys: I2RKey || R2IKey || AttestationChallenge := Crypto_KDF(Ke, [], 'SessionKeys', 3*CRYPTO_SYMMETRIC_KEY_LENGTH_BITS)
Initialize Local Message Counter per Section 4.6.1.1
Initialize Message Reception State: peer max_message_counter := 0
Store encryption keys for secure session
Responder will use R2IKey to encrypt, I2RKey to decrypt
Send PakeFinished: StatusReport(GeneralCode := SUCCESS, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := SESSION_ESTABLISHMENT_SUCCESS)
Clear intermediate cryptographic state (Ke, w0, L, pA, pB, cA, cB)
PASE session now active and ready for encrypted traffic
transitions.timing_requirements: Immediate session activation after cA verification
transitions.security_notes: Session keys derived from Ke via KDF with distinct purposes; AttestationChallenge enables secure device attestation; Ke cleared after derivation

actions: Set SessionTimestamp := current time (for session aging)
Derive session keys: I2RKey || R2IKey || AttestationChallenge := Crypto_KDF(Ke, [], 'SessionKeys', 3*CRYPTO_SYMMETRIC_KEY_LENGTH_BITS)
Initialize Local Message Counter per Section 4.6.1.1
Initialize Message Reception State: peer max_message_counter := 0
Store encryption keys for secure session
Initiator will use I2RKey to encrypt, R2IKey to decrypt
Clear intermediate cryptographic state (Ke, w0, w1, pA, pB, cA, cB)
NOW can send encrypted application data (commissioning commands)
transitions.timing_requirements: Immediate session activation upon PakeFinished receipt
transitions.security_notes: Session now secure; encrypted traffic can begin; both parties have mutually authenticated via passcode; Ke cleared after derivation
Prevents indefinite waiting; no cryptographic state compromised
Prevents indefinite waiting; PAKE verifier (w0, L) remains intact for next attempt
Must clear passcode-derived values to prevent leakage

actions: CRITICAL: Do NOT install session keys (cA not verified!)
Clear Ke, cA, cB (derived cryptographic values)
transitions.security_notes: CRITICAL: Must not install session keys before cA verification - prevents MITM with partial authentication

actions: Decision: May install keys locally (cB verified, responder likely received cA)
OR: Clear all cryptographic state and return to Idle
Recommended: Return to Idle for safety
If installing keys locally: Mark session as unconfirmed
Clear Ke, cA (derived values) if not installing
Clear pA, pB, w0, w1 in either case
transitions.security_notes: At this point, initiator has verified cB (responder knows passcode) and sent cA. Keys COULD be installed locally but responder state unknown.

guard: PBKDFParamRequest received matches previous request (same initiatorRandom, initiatorSessionId)
transitions.security_notes: Duplicate handling prevents unnecessary re-computation and provides idempotency

guard: Pake1 received matches previous Pake1 (same pA)
transitions.security_notes: Duplicate handling provides idempotency; no recomputation of SPAKE2+ values

guard: Pake2 received matches previous Pake2 (same pB, cB)
transitions.security_notes: Duplicate handling provides idempotency for retransmissions

guard: Pake3 received matches previous Pake3 (same cA)
transitions.security_notes: Duplicate handling for reliable message delivery; session already established

guard: TLV parsing failed OR required field missing OR invalid field size
actions: Send StatusReport: GeneralCode := FAILURE, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := INVALID_PARAMETER
transitions.security_notes: Early rejection of malformed messages prevents processing attacks

guard: Proposed Session ID (InitiatorSessionId or ResponderSessionId) conflicts with existing session
actions: Send StatusReport: GeneralCode := FAILURE, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := INVALID_PARAMETER
Initiator may retry with different Session ID
transitions.security_notes: Prevents session confusion attacks

guard: Parameters differ from QR code (if HasPBKDFParameters was True)
actions: Send StatusReport: GeneralCode := FAILURE, ProtocolId := SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode := INVALID_PARAMETER
transitions.security_notes: Validates PBKDF parameters are within acceptable security bounds

messages.description: PBKDF parameter request from initiator (Commissioner) to responder (Commissionee)

fields.description: Random number generated by initiator via Crypto_DRBG for transcript freshness
Unique session identifier chosen by initiator for this PASE session
Passcode identifier (0 for commissioning window verifier, non-zero reserved)
True if initiator knows PBKDF params (from QR code), False if responder must provide
Optional session parameters containing tags 1-9 (see session_parameters section)
contains: Tag 2: SESSIONACTIVEINTERVAL (optional, REQUIRED if tags 3-9 present)
Tag 9: MAXTCPMESSAGESIZE (REQUIRED if tag 8 has TCP bit set)

session_parameter_checks: - If any tag > 2 present, tag 2 MUST be present
- If tag 8 has TCP bit, tag 9 MUST be present
security_checks: No existing session with same initiatorSessionId
Ephemeral node ID not colliding with ongoing sessions

messages.security_notes: Sent unencrypted; initiatorRandom contributes to transcript to prevent replay

messages.description: PBKDF parameter response from responder to initiator with salt, iterations

fields.description: Echo of initiator's random for transcript binding
Random number generated by responder via Crypto_DRBG for transcript freshness
Unique session identifier chosen by responder for this PASE session
PBKDF salt and iteration count (only if hasPBKDFParameters was False in request)
Optional session parameters containing tags 1-9 (see session_parameters section)
contains: Tag 2: SESSIONACTIVEINTERVAL (optional, REQUIRED if tags 3-9 present)
Tag 9: MAXTCPMESSAGESIZE (REQUIRED if tag 8 has TCP bit set)

conditional_field_checks: If hasPBKDFParameters was False in request:
If hasPBKDFParameters was True in request:
session_parameter_checks: - If any tag > 2 present, tag 2 MUST be present
- Apply backwards compatibility defaults if tags missing

messages.security_notes: PBKDF params not secret; echo of initiatorRandom binds to request

messages.description: First PAKE message from initiator with public point pA

fields.description: Initiator's SPAKE2+ public point computed as pA = w0*P + M (uncompressed format)

ecc_checks: pA is valid point on P-256 curve

messages.security_notes: pA reveals no passcode info due to SPAKE2+ blinding with random point M. ECC validation prevents small-subgroup attacks.

messages.description: Second PAKE message from responder with pB and confirmation cB

fields.description: Responder's SPAKE2+ public point computed as pB = w0*P + N (uncompressed format)
Responder's confirmation value: cB = HMAC(KcB, pA), proves responder knows passcode

ecc_checks: pB is valid point on P-256 curve

messages.security_notes: cB is MAC over pA using key derived from shared secret; only correct passcode yields matching cB. MUST use constant-time comparison.

messages.description: Third PAKE message from initiator with confirmation cA

fields.description: Initiator's confirmation value: cA = HMAC(KcA, pB), proves initiator knows passcode

messages.security_notes: cA is MAC over pB; mutual authentication completed after both cA and cB verified. MUST use constant-time comparison.

messages.description: Session establishment success confirmation from responder

fields.description: GeneralCode=SUCCESS, ProtocolId=SECURE_CHANNEL, ProtocolCode=SESSION_ESTABLISHMENT_SUCCESS

messages.security_notes: Signals to initiator that responder has verified cA and installed session keys; after this, encrypted traffic can begin

cryptographic_functions.description: Deterministic Random Bit Generator for generating random values
Password-Based Key Derivation Function to derive w0, w1 from passcode
cryptographic_functions.usage: Derive PAKE values from passcode, salt, iterations
cryptographic_functions.formula: w0s || w1s = PBKDF(passcode_LE_32bit, salt, iterations, 2*(CRYPTO_GROUP_SIZE_BYTES+8)*8); w0 = w0s mod p; w1 = w1s mod p

cryptographic_functions.usage: pA = w0*P + M where M is fixed SPAKE2+ point, P is curve generator
security_requirements: Result must be valid EC point
Result must not equal M, N, P, or point at infinity

cryptographic_functions.usage: pB = w0*P + N where N is fixed SPAKE2+ point
security_requirements: Result must be valid EC point
Result must not equal M, N, P, or point at infinity

cryptographic_functions.description: SPAKE2+ transcript computation for confirmation values
cryptographic_functions.usage: TT = transcript(Context, M, N, pA, pB, Z, V, w0)
cryptographic_functions.exact_formula: TT = Context || 0x0000000000000000 || 0x0000000000000000 || lenM || M || lenN || N || lenpA || pA || lenpB || pB || lenZ || Z || lenV || V || lenw0 || w0
cryptographic_functions.context_computation: Context = Hash('CHIP PAKE V1 Commissioning' || PBKDFParamRequest || PBKDFParamResponse)

cryptographic_functions.description: SPAKE2+ confirmation computation and key derivation
cryptographic_functions.formula: KcA, KcB, Ke derived from SPAKE2+ shared secret; cA = HMAC(KcA, pB), cB = HMAC(KcB, pA)

cryptographic_functions.description: HKDF-based Key Derivation Function for session keys
cryptographic_functions.formula: Uses HKDF with inputKey=Ke, salt=[], info='SessionKeys', derives 3 symmetric keys (each 128 bits)

cryptographic_functions.usage: cA and cB verification to prevent timing attacks
cryptographic_functions.implementation: result = 0; for i in 0..31: result |= a[i] XOR b[i]; return result == 0
security_requirements: All bytes must be compared regardless of result
Compilation must not optimize away constant-time property

security_properties.description: Both initiator and responder must prove knowledge of passcode before session establishment
security_properties.verification_method: Verify cB at initiator (proves R knows passcode) AND verify cA at responder (proves I knows passcode)
security_properties.attack_prevented: Impersonation attacks - no party can establish session without correct passcode

security_properties.description: Derived session keys (I2RKey, R2IKey) remain secret from adversary without passcode
security_properties.formalization: SessionEstablished(I, R) AND NOT PasscodeCompromised ==> NOT Attacker_Knows(I2RKey) AND NOT Attacker_Knows(R2IKey) AND NOT Attacker_Knows(Ke)
security_properties.verification_method: Prove session keys are computationally infeasible to derive without knowing passcode (SPAKE2+ security)
security_properties.attack_prevented: Eavesdropping - passive attacker cannot decrypt session traffic

security_properties.description: Session keys remain secret even if passcode is later compromised (limited forward secrecy)
security_properties.formalization: SessionEstablished(I, R, t1) AND PasscodeCompromised(t2) WHERE t2 > t1 ==> Session keys at t1 remain computationally hard to recover
security_properties.verification_method: SPAKE2+ provides computational forward secrecy; session keys derived from ephemeral ECDH components
security_properties.attack_prevented: Past session compromise after passcode leakage
security_properties.limitation: Computational not perfect forward secrecy (depends on discrete log hardness)

security_properties.description: Transcript integrity ensures all messages in PASE exchange are authentic and unmodified
security_properties.formalization: cA_verified AND cB_verified ==> Transcript(PBKDFParamRequest, PBKDFParamResponse, pA, pB) matches at both parties
security_properties.verification_method: Confirmation values cA, cB are HMACs over transcript including all prior messages

security_properties.description: Random nonces (InitiatorRandom, ResponderRandom) prevent replay of PASE exchanges
security_properties.formalization: Two PASE sessions cannot have identical (InitiatorRandom, ResponderRandom) pairs
security_properties.verification_method: 256-bit DRBG randoms in transcript make each session cryptographically unique
security_properties.attack_prevented: Replay attacks - old PASE exchanges cannot be replayed

security_properties.description: Session identifiers (InitiatorSessionId, ResponderSessionId) uniquely bind PASE to subsequent encrypted session
security_properties.formalization: SessionEstablished(SessionID_I, SessionID_R) ==> All encrypted traffic uses exactly these session IDs
security_properties.verification_method: Session IDs chosen uniquely and stored in session context; encrypted messages reference session ID

security_properties.description: Invalid passcode causes PASE to fail before any session keys are derived
security_properties.formalization: Passcode_Initiator != Passcode_Responder ==> (cB_verification_fails OR cA_verification_fails) AND NOT SessionEstablished
security_properties.verification_method: Confirmation value mismatch detected at cB (T5a) or cA (T6a) verification
security_properties.attack_prevented: Brute-force passcode guessing - each failed attempt detected immediately

security_properties.description: AttestationChallenge derived from Ke is fresh for each PASE session
security_properties.formalization: SessionEstablished(session1) AND SessionEstablished(session2) WHERE session1 != session2 ==> AttestationChallenge(session1) != AttestationChallenge(session2)
security_properties.verification_method: Ke is unique per session (derived from unique pA, pB via ECDH); KDF with 'SessionKeys' info derives unique AttestationChallenge
security_properties.attack_prevented: Replay of Device Attestation responses across sessions

security_properties.description: Initiator must not send encrypted data before receiving PakeFinished from responder
security_properties.formalization: Initiator_Encrypted_Send(msg, t) ==> EXISTS t_finished WHERE ReceivePakeFinished(t_finished) AND t_finished < t
security_properties.verification_method: Protocol specification constraint in T8 transition; initiator waits for PakeFinished
security_properties.attack_prevented: Premature encryption before mutual authentication complete

security_properties.description: SPAKE2+ prevents offline dictionary attacks on passcode from eavesdropped PASE exchanges
security_properties.formalization: Attacker observes (PBKDFParamRequest, PBKDFParamResponse, pA, pB, cA, cB) ==> Cannot efficiently test passcode candidates offline
security_properties.verification_method: SPAKE2+ blinding (M, N points) prevents extraction of w0, w1 from pA, pB without solving discrete log
security_properties.attack_prevented: Offline brute-force on passcode from captured network traffic
security_properties.security_assumption: Elliptic curve discrete logarithm problem hardness (P-256)

security_properties.description: Failed cB/cA verification does not leak partial PAKE state that could aid passcode guessing
security_properties.formalization: cB_verification_fails OR cA_verification_fails ==> Attacker learns only 'wrong passcode', not partial match information
security_properties.verification_method: Confirmation values are HMAC outputs; partial matches don't provide information about passcode
security_properties.attack_prevented: Partial information leakage attacks on passcode
security_properties.implementation_requirement: Clear all intermediate state after failure

security_properties.description: Session IDs are isolated per session and cannot be used for cross-session attacks
security_properties.formalization: Session(ID1, keys1) AND Session(ID2, keys2) WHERE ID1 != ID2 ==> keys1 isolated from keys2
security_properties.verification_method: Session IDs are 16-bit unique identifiers per party; session context includes session ID in encryption

security_properties.description: Cryptographic comparisons (cA, cB verification) must be constant-time to prevent timing side-channels
security_properties.formalization: Time(verify(cB, cB_computed)) is independent of position of first differing byte
security_properties.verification_method: Constant-time comparison algorithm: result = 0; for i in 0..31: result |= a[i] XOR b[i]; return result == 0
security_properties.attack_prevented: Timing side-channel attacks on passcode verification
security_properties.vulnerable_code: for i in 0..31: if cB_received[i] != cB_computed[i]: return FALSE
security_properties.correct_code: result = 0; for i in 0..31: result |= cB_received[i] XOR cB_computed[i]; return result == 0
security_properties.impact_without_mitigation: Timing leak ~1-2 bits per attempt; for 20-27 bit passcode: reduces to ~10-15 bits effective entropy

security_properties.description: PBKDF iteration bounds prevent CPU exhaustion DoS attacks
security_properties.formalization: iterations in [1000, 100000] ==> PBKDF computation bounded to reasonable time
security_properties.verification_method: Validate iteration count before starting PBKDF computation
security_properties.attack_prevented: CPU exhaustion via malicious PBKDF parameters (e.g., iterations = MAX_INT)

security_properties.description: Message counters after session establishment are monotonically increasing to prevent replay
security_properties.formalization: Message(counter=n, session) received ==> All future messages have counter > n for same session
security_properties.verification_method: Message Reception State tracks max_message_counter per session
security_properties.attack_prevented: Replay attacks on encrypted session traffic

failure_modes.handling: Responder sends FAILURE StatusReport, terminates exchange (T2a)
failure_modes.recovery: None - initiator must retry with passcodeId = 0

failure_modes.handling: Initiator sends FAILURE StatusReport, terminates exchange (T5a)
failure_modes.recovery: None - indicates wrong passcode; commissioning must restart with correct passcode
failure_modes.security_implication: Prevents session establishment with incorrect passcode

failure_modes.handling: Responder sends FAILURE StatusReport, terminates exchange (T6a)
failure_modes.recovery: None - indicates wrong passcode or MITM attack
failure_modes.security_implication: Prevents rogue initiator from establishing session

failure_modes.handling: MRP retransmission up to max retries
failure_modes.recovery: If retries exhausted, PASE fails; restart from Idle

failure_modes.trigger: No response within expected timeframe (30 seconds per message)
failure_modes.handling: Clear cryptographic state, transition to Idle

failure_modes.trigger: ECC point validation failure, PBKDF overflow, etc.
failure_modes.handling: Send FAILURE StatusReport, clear state, terminate

failure_modes.trigger: TLV parsing failure, missing required field, invalid size
Proposed Session ID already in use
Iterations out of bounds or salt too short

implementation_notes.details: High iteration counts (e.g., 10000+) can cause multi-second delays at T3 transition; implementations should display progress indicator
Passcode typically 6-8 decimal digits (20-27 bits entropy); SPAKE2+ prevents offline attacks but online rate-limiting REQUIRED
recommendations: Clear commissioning window after 5 consecutive failures
Log failed attempts for security monitoring

implementation_notes.details: PASE sessions are ephemeral (commissioning only); after commissioning complete, CASE (certificate-based) sessions replace PASE
destruction_procedure: 1. Securely clear I2RKey, R2IKey, Ke from memory (zeroize)
5. DO NOT log keys or key material

implementation_notes.details: Derived challenge used in Section 6.2.3 Device Attestation Procedure immediately after PASE; prevents replay of attestation responses
NIST P-256 (secp256r1) required; M and N points from SPAKE2+ draft v2 specification hardcoded
All PASE message payloads are TLV-encoded per Matter TLV specification; unknown context tags silently ignored for forward compatibility
cA and cB verification MUST use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks on passcode validation
implementation_notes.vulnerable_pattern: Early-exit comparison: if cB[i] != computed[i]: return FALSE
implementation_notes.correct_pattern: Constant-time: result = 0; for i in 0..31: result |= a[i] XOR b[i]; return result == 0
implementation_notes.alternative_pattern: HMAC-based: return HMAC(K, cB_received) == HMAC(K, cB_computed)

implementation_notes.details: Passcode for commissioning window should be displayed only when window is open
recommendations: ONLY show passcode while window open
Use QR code for passcode + PBKDF params
Never show passcode on device startup
Close window immediately after successful commissioning
Multiple windows: Basic method (1 passcode) vs Enhanced (per-window passcode)

implementation_notes.details: Salt and iteration count selection impacts security and performance
salt_requirements: Public (included in QR code or sent in response)
Generated from hardware RNG during manufacturing

implementation_notes.details: Cache outgoing messages for idempotent retransmission on duplicate requests

cross_references.title: HMAC (Keyed-Hash Message Authentication Code)
cross_references.integration: Used for cA, cB confirmation value computation

cross_references.title: KDF (Key Derivation Function)
cross_references.integration: Used for I2RKey, R2IKey, AttestationChallenge derivation

cross_references.title: PBKDF (Password-Based Key Derivation Function)
cross_references.integration: Used for w0, w1 derivation from passcode

cross_references.title: PAKE (Password-Authenticated Key Exchange) - SPAKE2+
cross_references.integration: Core protocol for PASE key exchange

cross_references.title: Message Frame Format
cross_references.integration: All PASE messages use this format

cross_references.title: Message Counter Initialization
cross_references.integration: Message counters initialized after session establishment

cross_references.title: Message Reliability Protocol (MRP)
cross_references.integration: All PASE messages require reliable transmission
cross_references.missing_details: MRP FSM integration, timeout parameters per message type

cross_references.title: Device Attestation Procedure
cross_references.boundary: Device Attestation FSM starts after SessionEstablished state

session_parameters.description: Session parameter fields exchanged in initiatorSessionParams and responderSessionParams (Section 4.13.2)

fields.name: SESSIONIDLEINTERVAL
fields.description: Maximum idle interval (ms) before session considered inactive

fields.name: SESSIONACTIVEINTERVAL
fields.description: Maximum active interval (ms) before session must be closed
fields.backwards_compatibility: SHALL be present if any tag after 2 is present

fields.name: SESSIONACTIVETHRESHOLD
fields.description: Threshold for active interval counting

fields.name: DATAMODELREVISION
fields.description: Data model revision (17, 18, etc.)

fields.name: INTERACTIONMODELREVISION
fields.description: Interaction model revision (11, 12, etc.)

fields.name: SPECIFICATIONVERSION
fields.description: Matter specification version (e.g., 0x01040000 for 1.4)

fields.name: MAXPATHSPERINVOKE
fields.description: Maximum paths per Invoke command

fields.name: SUPPORTEDTRANSPORTS
fields.description: Bitmap of supported transports (bit 0: MRP, bit 1: TCP)

fields.name: MAXTCPMESSAGESIZE
fields.description: Maximum TCP message size if TCP transport supported
fields.conditional: MUST be present if SUPPORTEDTRANSPORTS has TCP bit set

validation_rules: Tags MUST appear in ascending order (STRUCTURE tag-order)
If any tag after tag 2 is present, tag 2 (SESSIONACTIVEINTERVAL) SHALL also be present
If tag 8 has TCP bit set, tag 9 MUST be present
Unknown tags SHALL be silently ignored for forward compatibility

cryptographic_constants.description: Exact cryptographic constant values from Matter specification

spake2_plus.point_format: Compressed (33 bytes) or Uncompressed (65 bytes, 0x04 prefix)

transcript.description: Exact byte structure for SPAKE2+ transcript (Section 3.10.3)
transcript.formula: TT = Context || 0x0000000000000000 || 0x0000000000000000 || lenM || M || lenN || N || lenpA || pA || lenpB || pB || lenZ || Z || lenV || V || lenw0 || w0
transcript.context: Hash('CHIP PAKE V1 Commissioning' || PBKDFParamRequest || PBKDFParamResponse)

nonce_construction.description: Nonce for encrypted messages after session establishment (Section 4.8.1.1)
nonce_construction.format: Nonce = SecurityFlags (1 byte) || MessageCounter (4 bytes, LE) || SourceNodeID (8 bytes, LE)

bit_7_P_flag.name: Privacy
bit_7_P_flag.description: MUST be 0 for PASE (no privacy yet)

PBKDFParamRequest.description: Source Node ID present, no Dest Node ID

PBKDFParamResponse.description: No Source, Dest Node ID present (64-bit)

session_id_exhaustion.handling: Node SHALL terminate an existing session to free an identifier (Section 4.13.2.4)

ephemeral_node_id.description: Initiator generates random ephemeral node ID for PASE (Section 4.13.2)
ephemeral_node_id.uniqueness: MUST NOT collide with other ongoing sessions
ephemeral_node_id.tracking: Responder tracks as Peer Session Identifier
ephemeral_node_id.lifetime: Valid only for PASE session duration

message_counter_wraparound.handling: Session MUST be terminated before counter wraps (Section 4.6.1.1)
message_counter_wraparound.prevention: Close session after 2^31 messages as safety margin

tlv_tag_ordering.rule: STRUCTURE tag-order requires tags in ascending order

pbkdf_parameters.if_true: pbkdf_parameters MUST NOT be present in response
pbkdf_parameters.if_false: pbkdf_parameters MUST be present in response

backwards_compatibility.description: Rules for interoperability with older Matter versions

rules.description: If any tag after tag 2 is present in session-parameter-struct, tag 2 (SESSIONACTIVEINTERVAL) SHALL also be present
rules.rationale: Older devices expect tag 2 if newer tags present

rules.description: If tag 4 (DATAMODELREVISION) absent, device is pre-Matter 1.3
If tag 5 (INTERACTIONMODELREVISION) absent, device is pre-Matter 1.3
If tag 6 (SPECIFICATIONVERSION) absent, device is pre-Matter 1.3
Silently ignore unknown context-specific tags for forward compatibility
rules.behavior: Parse and skip unknown tags without error
